description:
You are the site administrator of a small on-premise server.  
To prepare two new employees for their first day you must:

1. Finish the central personnel file  
   • Path: /home/user/site_admin/new_hires.csv  
   • The file already exists and currently contains **only** the header line  
     username,uid,group,shell,home_dir,required_packages  
   • Append **exactly** two new data rows for the users shown below.  
     Data must be written in the **same order** as the header and the values are **comma-separated, no spaces**.  
     a) alice → 1001, staff, /bin/bash, /home/alice, bash;curl  
     b) bob   → 1002, developers, /bin/zsh, /home/bob, nano;cowsay  

2. Prepare each user’s working directory  
   • Create /home/user/site_admin/users/alice/scripts/  
   • Create /home/user/site_admin/users/bob/scripts/  
   • Inside /home/user/site_admin/users/alice/ write a file called README.txt that contains exactly  
     This directory belongs to alice.  
   • Inside /home/user/site_admin/users/bob/ write a file called README.txt that contains exactly  
     This directory belongs to bob.  

3. Produce a package-availability audit  
   • A local “inventory” file already exists at /home/user/site_admin/packages_database.txt.  
     Each line is in the form  
       <package_name>,<version_string>  
     Example: bash,5.1-2  
   • Using that inventory, decide for every package required by the two new users whether it is already present.  
   • Write the results to /home/user/site_admin/package_audit.log with **one line per package** in the exact format  
       username,package,status  
     where status is the single word installed or missing.  
   • Lines must be sorted first by username (alice before bob) and then alphabetically by package name.  
     Therefore the finished log must contain **exactly** these four lines IN THIS ORDER:  
       alice,bash,installed  
       alice,curl,installed  
       bob,cowsay,missing  
       bob,nano,installed  

When you are done the automated checker will verify:  
• new_hires.csv contains the two new rows with correct comma placement and no extra whitespace.  
• The users/<name>/scripts/ directories and README.txt files exist with the exact content.  
• package_audit.log exists in the required path and its four lines match the specification precisely.  

Do NOT delete or rename any existing files. Work only inside /home/user/site_admin/ and its sub-directories.
truth:
Initial state (before the agent starts):

Directory tree:
/home/user/site_admin/
/home/user/site_admin/new_hires.csv      (one line: "username,uid,group,shell,home_dir,required_packages\n")
/home/user/site_admin/packages_database.txt  (contents below)
/home/user/site_admin/users/              (empty directory)

Contents of /home/user/site_admin/packages_database.txt  (exact lines):
bash,5.1-2
coreutils,8.32-4
nano,5.4-2
curl,7.74.0-1
python3,3.9.2-1

Expected final state after correct completion:

/home/user/site_admin/new_hires.csv  (exact 3 lines, newline at end)
username,uid,group,shell,home_dir,required_packages
alice,1001,staff,/bin/bash,/home/alice,bash;curl
bob,1002,developers,/bin/zsh,/home/bob,nano;cowsay

/home/user/site_admin/users/alice/README.txt  => "This directory belongs to alice.\n"
/home/user/site_admin/users/alice/scripts/    => directory exists (can be empty)
/home/user/site_admin/users/bob/README.txt    => "This directory belongs to bob.\n"
/home/user/site_admin/users/bob/scripts/      => directory exists (can be empty)

/home/user/site_admin/package_audit.log  (exact 4 lines, newline at end)
alice,bash,installed
alice,curl,installed
bob,cowsay,missing
bob,nano,installed
